resources.string: Loading your local experience and choosing the right starting point.
A calm, local-first space for psychological self-reflection through the ten sephirot.
Onboarding, navigation, bilingual structure, and placeholders for the flows we will build next.
Review saved runs and compare future retakes once persistence is wired in.
Read the deeper context behind the Tree of Life in psychologically grounded language.
Prepare for English and Spanish support, privacy framing, and future local preferences.
A reflective map, not a label
This app uses the Kabbalah Tree of Life as a structure for self-observation. It is designed to help you notice patterns, strengths, and edges in your current experience.
Each sephira is explored as a living tendency. Results describe whether an area currently feels more balanced, underexpressed, or overextended. They are not fixed identity statements.
The core experience works offline and stores progress locally on your device. The interface is ready for both English and Spanish, following your device language.
Expect a brief introduction now, then a clearer assessment flow in the next build.
The questionnaire and sephira intros come next. This route already exists so we can connect the app shell before scoring and question content are finalized.
This screen will evolve into the full Tree of Life profile with ten sephirot, softened confidence language, and deeper interpretation.
Completed assessments and retakes will appear here once local persistence for sessions and results is added.
This area will hold the richer educational content adapted from the source document, separate from the short onboarding flow.
The shell is ready for language-aware UI, privacy guidance, and future local preferences. For now, app language follows the device locale automatically.
English and Spanish strings are both available. More settings can be added later without changing the navigation structure.
Ready for the next feature slice
